title: Define and Call a Function
instruction: Define a function `greet(name string) string` that returns `"Hello, " + name + "!"`. Call it with "Alice" and print the result.
hint: func greet(name string) string { return "Hello, " + name + "!" }

title: Parameters and Return Values
instruction: Define `add(a int, b int) int` that returns the sum of the two numbers. Call it with 4 and 6, then print the result.
hint: Write `func add(a int, b int) int { return a + b }`.

title: Multiple Return Values
instruction: Define `minMax(nums []int) (int, int)` that returns the smallest and largest number in the slice. Call it with `[]int{3, 1, 4, 1, 5}` and print both results.
hint: Start `min` and `max` with `nums[0]`, then compare the rest inside a loop.

title: Variadic Function
instruction: Define `sum(nums ...int) int` that returns the total of all arguments. Call it with 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 and print the result.
hint: Loop over `nums` and keep a running total.

title: Use a Helper Function
instruction: Define a helper function `isEven(n int) bool` that returns true when a number is even. Call it with 8 and print the result.
hint: An even number has remainder 0 when divided by 2.
